message.content:
Read `./.claude/progress.txt`, pick the most important pending task, execute it, update the file, commit, and stop.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT change logic, only migrate structure
- The source of truth is card-place, consult it only if in doubt
- The code has already been copied to this repository
- Everything must be migrated to the new architecture/Style Guide (./.claude/STYLE_GUIDE.md)

WORKFLOW:
1. Read progress.txt and identify the most important pending single task
2. If NO pending tasks exist, signal completion (see OUTPUT STATUS below) and stop
3. If there is a task:
   a. Update task status to "in progress" in progress.txt
   b. Execute migration following STYLE_GUIDE.md
   c. Launch 10 review agent to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   d. Review output and consider improvements
   e. Implement corrections if needed
   f. Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   g. Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   h. Output status block (see below)
   i. Stop

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

CRITICAL (THINK BEFORE ACTING):
1. The FIRST thing you MUST do is write your understanding of each WORKFLOW statement
2. The SECOND thing you MUST do is write your understanding of the work in progress described in progress.txt
3. Only AFTER completing steps 1 and 2, proceed with the task execution
4. If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
5. Only set "exit": true when ALL tasks in progress.txt are completed

   143→## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED
   144→

   146→File created at src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts with all required functionality.

   148→### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
   149→Added: `export * from './usePoints';`
   150→
   151→This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.
   152→
   153→```typescript
   154→import { useCallback } from 'react';
   155→import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
   156→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   157→import {
   158→  getPointsProducts,
   159→  clearPointsProducts,
   160→  getPointsBalance,
   161→  getParameterPointsProgram,
   162→} from '../services';
   163→import {
   164→  PointsProductsStatus,
   165→  PointsBalanceStatus,
   166→  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
   167→} from '../models';
   168→
   169→export function usePoints() {
   170→  // Products from products store
   171→  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
   172→  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();
   173→
   174→  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
   175→  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));
   176→
   177→  // Parameterization from parameter store
   178→  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
   179→    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
   180→  );
   181→
   182→  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
   183→    await Promise.all([
   184→      getPointsBalance(),
   185→      getPointsProducts(),
   186→      getParameterPointsProgram(),
   187→    ]);
   188→  }, []);
   189→
   190→  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
   191→    await getPointsBalance();
   192→  }, []);
   193→
   194→  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
   195→    await getPointsProducts();
   196→  }, []);
   197→
   198→  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
   199→    await getParameterPointsProgram();
   200→  }, []);
   201→
   202→  const clear = useCallback(() => {
   203→    clearPointsProducts();
   204→  }, []);
   205→
   206→  return {
   207→    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
   208→    points: pointsBalance.points,
   209→    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
   210→    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,
   211→
   212→    // Products (from products store)
   213→    products: pointsProducts.products,
   214→    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
   215→    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,
   216→
   217→    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
   218→    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
   219→    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
   220→    isLoadingParameterization:
   221→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
   222→    isErrorParameterization:
   223→      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,
   224→
   225→    // Actions
   226→    requestPointsBalance,
   227→    requestPointsProducts,
   228→    requestParameterization,
   229→    requestInitialData,
   230→    clear,
   231→  };
   232→}
   233→```

   258→import { useCallback } from 'react';

   260→import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
   261→import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';

   263→
   264→export function useMarketplace() {
   265→  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

   267→  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
   268→    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
   269→      await getMarketplace(params);
   270→    },
   271→    []
   272→  );
   273→
   274→  const clear = useCallback(() => {
   275→    clearMarketplace();
   276→  }, []);
   277→
   278→  return {
   279→    data: marketplace.data,
   280→    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
   281→    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
   282→    requestGetMarketplace,
   283→    clear,
   284→  };
   285→}
   286→```

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

Last Updated: 2026-01-26 22:55
Status: INCOMPLETE - SCREEN MIGRATION IN PROGRESS (5/6 screens migrated)

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (VERIFIED 2026-01-26)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure: COMPLETE

================================================================================
STATE MAPPING: Redux → Zustand
================================================================================

## pointsReducer (Redux) → Multiple Zustand stores

| Redux Field                    | Zustand Location                              | Service Function           |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------|
| points                         | pointsBalance.points (extract store)          | getPointsBalance()         |
| isLoadingBalance               | pointsBalance.status === LOADING              | -                          |
| isErrorBalance                 | pointsBalance.status === ERROR                | -                          |
| products                       | pointsProducts.products (products store)      | getPointsProducts()        |
| isLoadingProducts              | pointsProducts.status === LOADING             | -                          |

| isErrorParameterization        | parameterPointsProgram.status === ERROR       | -                          |

| appliedExpiringFilters         | pointsExtractFilters.appliedExpiringFilters   | -                          |

## rescueReducer (Redux) → rescue store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location            | Service Function |
|-------------|-----------------------------|------------------|
| isLoading   | rescue.status === LOADING   | performRescue()  |
| isError     | rescue.status === ERROR     | -                |
| status      | rescue.responseStatus       | -                |

## getMarketplaceReducer (Redux) → marketplace store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location              | Service Function  |
|-------------|-------------------------------|-------------------|
| isLoading   | marketplace.status === LOADING| getMarketplace()  |

## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 2.1 Create src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts with all required functionality.

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
Added: `export * from './usePoints';`

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

### 2.2 Verify ParameterPointsProgramStatus exists
File: src/services/models/parameter.model.ts

Should have:
```typescript

  IDLE = 'idle',
  LOADING = 'loading',

}
```

If missing, add it.

File created at src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts with all required functionality.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.  
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.  
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.  
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.  
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.  
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.  
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).  
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.  
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.  
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.  
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.  
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:  
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)  
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**  
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**  
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**  
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**  
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**  
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**  
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**  
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**  
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**  
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**  
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.  
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→
   507→Happy shipping! 🚀

# Reward Program - Redux to Zustand Migration

Last Updated: 2026-01-26 22:55
Status: INCOMPLETE - SCREEN MIGRATION IN PROGRESS (5/6 screens migrated)

================================================================================
CRITICAL: PREVIOUS SESSION ERRORS TO AVOID
================================================================================

1. INFRASTRUCTURE WAS CREATED BUT SCREENS WERE NOT MIGRATED
   - Zustand stores exist but screens still use Redux connect()
   - useRescue hook was created but NEVER integrated into screens
   - This made the migration USELESS - data flows through Redux, not Zustand

2. TYPES WERE DUPLICATED UNNECESSARILY
   - PointsProduct already exists in services/models/products.model.ts
   - Do NOT add duplicate types - check if they exist first

3. BALANCE LOCATION WAS CONFUSED
   - Balance is in `pointsBalance` within extract.store.ts
   - NOT in parameter.store.ts
   - The getPointsBalance() service exists in extract.service.ts

4. PRODUCTION PATTERN WAS NOT FOLLOWED
   - Production (card-place) calls services DIRECTLY, not always through hooks
   - Example: getParameterPointsProgram() is called directly in Points screen

5. usePermission USES SuperApp's Redux (customerReducer.nrc)
   - This is NOT local Redux to migrate
   - Production also uses useSelector for this
   - Keep as-is until SuperApp migrates customer data to Zustand

================================================================================
CURRENT STATE (VERIFIED 2026-01-26)
================================================================================

## Zustand Infrastructure: COMPLETE

| Domain       | Store | API | Model | Repository | Service | Hook   |
|--------------|-------|-----|-------|------------|---------|--------|
| airlineMiles | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | -          | ✅      | ✅     |
| extract      | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | ✅         | ✅      | -      |
| parameter    | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | ✅         | ✅      | -      |
| permission   | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | ✅         | ✅      | ✅     |
| products     | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | ✅         | ✅      | -      |
| rescue       | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | -          | ✅      | ✅ (1) |
| marketplace  | ✅    | ✅  | ✅    | -          | ✅      | -      |

(1) useRescue exists but is NEVER USED by any screen

## Hooks Status:
- src/services/hooks/useAirlineMiles.ts - ✅ Used by AirlineMilesMarketplace
- src/services/hooks/usePermission.ts - ✅ Used by InvoiceCashbackSummary
- src/services/hooks/useRescue.ts - ❌ EXISTS BUT UNUSED
- src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts - ✅ USED by Points screen
- src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts - ✅ CREATED (Phase 3 completed)

## Screens Status:

| Screen                    | Uses Redux? | Uses Zustand? | Migration Status |
|---------------------------|-------------|---------------|------------------|
| AirlineMilesMarketplace   | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| ExtractRewardProgram      | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| LearnMoreRewardProgram    | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| MyOrdersRewardProgram     | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| Points                    | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| PointsDetail              | No          | N/A           | ✅ DONE          |
| RewardProgramMarketplace  | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| InvoiceCashbackOnboarding | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| InvoiceCashbackPoints     | No          | Yes           | ✅ DONE          |
| InvoiceCashbackSummary    | YES         | Partial (4)   | ❌ PENDING       |

(2) Points uses usePoints hook (migrated 2026-01-26)
(3) PointsDetail - dead Redux code removed (2026-01-26)
(4) InvoiceCashbackSummary uses usePermission (Zustand) but rescueActions (Redux)

## Redux Files That MUST BE DELETED (after screen migration):

- src/redux/ducks/points.ts
- src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts
- src/redux/ducks/getMarketplace.ts
- src/redux/reducers/Models/index.ts
- src/redux/reducers/index.ts
- src/redux/sagas/points/models/index.ts (only contains ParametrizationData type)

## Missing Types in Zustand Models:

- FilterOption - ✅ MIGRATED to services/models/products.model.ts (Phase 1 completed)

================================================================================
STATE MAPPING: Redux → Zustand
================================================================================

## pointsReducer (Redux) → Multiple Zustand stores

| Redux Field                    | Zustand Location                              | Service Function           |
|--------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|----------------------------|
| points                         | pointsBalance.points (extract store)          | getPointsBalance()         |
| isLoadingBalance               | pointsBalance.status === LOADING              | -                          |
| isErrorBalance                 | pointsBalance.status === ERROR                | -                          |
| products                       | pointsProducts.products (products store)      | getPointsProducts()        |
| isLoadingProducts              | pointsProducts.status === LOADING             | -                          |
| isErrorProducts                | pointsProducts.status === ERROR               | -                          |
| pointsQuantity                 | parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity         | getParameterPointsProgram()|
| conversionFactor               | parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor       | -                          |
| isLoadingParameterization      | parameterPointsProgram.status === LOADING     | -                          |
| isErrorParameterization        | parameterPointsProgram.status === ERROR       | -                          |
| appliedTransactionsFilters     | pointsExtractFilters.appliedTransactionsFilters | -                        |
| appliedExpiringFilters         | pointsExtractFilters.appliedExpiringFilters   | -                          |

## rescueReducer (Redux) → rescue store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location            | Service Function |
|-------------|-----------------------------|------------------|
| isLoading   | rescue.status === LOADING   | performRescue()  |
| isError     | rescue.status === ERROR     | -                |
| status      | rescue.responseStatus       | -                |

## getMarketplaceReducer (Redux) → marketplace store (Zustand)

| Redux Field | Zustand Location              | Service Function  |
|-------------|-------------------------------|-------------------|
| isLoading   | marketplace.status === LOADING| getMarketplace()  |
| isError     | marketplace.status === ERROR  | -                 |
| data        | marketplace.data              | -                 |

================================================================================
TASKS TO COMPLETE MIGRATION
================================================================================

## Phase 1: Add Missing Type [QUICK FIX] ✅ COMPLETED

### 1.1 Add FilterOption to products.model.ts ✅ DONE
File: src/services/models/products.model.ts
FilterOption interface added at end of file.

### 1.2 Export FilterOption from models/index.ts ✅ DONE
File: src/services/models/index.ts
Already exported via wildcard: `export * from './products.model'`

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Phase 2: Create usePoints Hook [CRITICAL] ✅ COMPLETED

### 2.1 Create src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/usePoints.ts with all required functionality.

### 2.2 Export from hooks/index.ts ✅ DONE
Added: `export * from './usePoints';`

This hook REPLACES all pointsReducer + PointsActions usage.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import {
  getPointsProducts,
  clearPointsProducts,
  getPointsBalance,
  getParameterPointsProgram,
} from '../services';
import {
  PointsProductsStatus,
  PointsBalanceStatus,
  ParameterPointsProgramStatus,
} from '../models';

export function usePoints() {
  // Products from products store
  const pointsProducts = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsProducts));
  const { pointsProductsActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  // Balance from extract store (pointsBalance)
  const pointsBalance = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.pointsBalance));

  // Parameterization from parameter store
  const parameterPointsProgram = useRootStore(
    useShallow((state) => state.parameterPointsProgram)
  );

  const requestInitialData = useCallback(async () => {
    await Promise.all([
      getPointsBalance(),
      getPointsProducts(),
      getParameterPointsProgram(),
    ]);
  }, []);

  const requestPointsBalance = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsBalance();
  }, []);

  const requestPointsProducts = useCallback(async () => {
    await getPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  const requestParameterization = useCallback(async () => {
    await getParameterPointsProgram();
  }, []);

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearPointsProducts();
  }, []);

  return {
    // Balance (from extract store - pointsBalance)
    points: pointsBalance.points,
    isLoadingBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorBalance: pointsBalance.status === PointsBalanceStatus.ERROR,

    // Products (from products store)
    products: pointsProducts.products,
    isLoadingProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorProducts: pointsProducts.status === PointsProductsStatus.ERROR,

    // Parameterization (from parameter store)
    pointsQuantity: parameterPointsProgram.pointsQuantity,
    conversionFactor: parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor,
    isLoadingParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.LOADING,
    isErrorParameterization:
      parameterPointsProgram.status === ParameterPointsProgramStatus.ERROR,

    // Actions
    requestPointsBalance,
    requestPointsProducts,
    requestParameterization,
    requestInitialData,
    clear,
  };
}
```

### 2.2 Verify ParameterPointsProgramStatus exists
File: src/services/models/parameter.model.ts

Should have:
```typescript
export enum ParameterPointsProgramStatus {
  IDLE = 'idle',
  LOADING = 'loading',
  SUCCESS = 'success',
  ERROR = 'error',
```

### 3.1 Create src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts ✅ DONE
File created at src/services/hooks/useMarketplace.ts with all required functionality.

```typescript
import { useCallback } from 'react';
import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { getMarketplace, clearMarketplace } from '../services';
import { MarketplaceStatus } from '../models';

export function useMarketplace() {
  const marketplace = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.marketplace));

  const requestGetMarketplace = useCallback(
    async (params: Record<string, string>) => {
      await getMarketplace(params);
    },
    []
  );

  const clear = useCallback(() => {
    clearMarketplace();
  }, []);

  return {
    data: marketplace.data,
    isLoading: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.LOADING,
    isError: marketplace.status === MarketplaceStatus.ERROR,
    requestGetMarketplace,
    clear,
  };
}
```

You are reviewing a Redux-to-Zustand migration for the InvoiceCashbackSummary screen.

**Context:**
- This is a React Native microfrontend for a Reward Program
- We migrated from Redux (connect HOC pattern) to Zustand hooks
- The migration should NOT change any logic, only structure

**Files changed:**
1. src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx - Main screen logic
2. src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/Models/index.ts - Type definitions

**Migration changes made:**
1. Removed Redux imports: connect, bindActionCreators, Dispatch, PointsActions, rescueActions, AppReducers
2. Added Zustand imports: usePoints, useRescue from services, RescuePayload type
3. Replaced props-based state (pointsReducer, rescueReducer) with hook-based state
4. Changed rescueRequest({ payload }) to rescueRequest(payload) - direct call
5. Changed from connect() HOC to direct export default
6. Updated Models/index.ts to use NativeStackScreenProps instead of Redux ScreenProps

**Your task:**
Read the migrated files and verify:
1. All Redux code has been properly removed
2. The Zustand hooks are being used correctly
3. No logic changes were made - only structural changes
4. Type definitions are correct

Please provide a brief assessment of the migration quality.

Is the usePoints hook being used correctly? Any issues?

Review the RescuePayload type usage in InvoiceCashbackSummary screen.

<result>Perfect. The rescueRequest function call signature has been correctly updated across all three files. Here's the verification:

## Call Signature Status: ✓ CORRECT

```typescript
const onConfirmRescue = () => {

  const payload: RescuePayload = {
    value: pointsSelected,
    campaignId: campaingId,

    description: 'Cashback resgatado',
  };
  rescueRequest(payload);  // ✓ Correct: Direct payload (new Zustand pattern)
};
```

### 2. **useRescue.ts** (Lines 11-16)
```typescript
const rescueRequest = useCallback(
  async (payload: RescuePayload, callbacks?: RescueCallbacks) => {
    await performRescue(payload, callbacks);  // ✓ Correct: Payload passed directly
  },
  []
);
```

### 3. **rescue.service.ts** (Line 6-9)
```typescript
export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  // ✓ Correct: Signature expects direct payload, not wrapped object
```

## Verification Summary

| Layer | Old Redux Pattern | New Zustand Pattern | Status |
|-------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|
| **Screen** | `rescueRequest({ payload })` | `rescueRequest(payload)` | ✓ |
| **Hook** | `performRescue({ payload })` | `performRescue(payload, callbacks?)` | ✓ |
| **Service** | Wrapped payload handling | Direct payload destructuring | ✓ |

The migration is complete and consistent. The screen correctly constructs the payload object and passes it directly to `rescueRequest()`, which forwards it to `performRescue()` without any wrapper nesting. This aligns with the modern Zustand pattern where the payload is passed as a direct argument rather than wrapped in Redux-style action objects.</result>

```typescript
useEffect(() => {
```

<summary>Agent "Review PropsModalizeRef usage" completed</summary>
<result>## Summary: PropsModalizeRef Type Usage Review

I've reviewed the modalizeRef type usage across the project. Here's what I found:

### Pattern Analysis

**Two distinct patterns exist in the codebase:**

#### Pattern 1: Type Assertion (InvoiceCashbackSummary, Points)
```typescript
// src/screens/InvoiceCashbackSummary/index.tsx - Line 130
const modalizeRef1 = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null as unknown as PropsModalizeRef);

// src/screens/Points/index.tsx - Line 25
const modalizeRef = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null as unknown as PropsModalizeRef);
```

#### Pattern 2: Simple null (PointsDetail, MyOrdersCashback)
```typescript
// src/screens/PointsDetail/index.tsx - Line 14
const modalizeRef = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null);

// src/screens/MyOrdersRewardProgram/index.tsx - Line 20
const modalizeRefFilters = useRef<PropsModalizeRef>(null);
```

#### Pattern 3: Component Props (PointsInfoBottomSheet, FiltersBottomSheet)
```typescript
// src/atomic/organisms/PointsInfoBottomSheet/index.tsx - Line 7
interface PointsInfoBottomSheetProps {
  modalizeRef: React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>;
}

// src/atomic/organisms/FiltersBottomSheet/index.tsx - Line 15
interface FiltersBottomSheetProps {
  modalizeRef: React.RefObject<PropsModalizeRef>;
}
```

### Usage Pattern (Consistent across all)
```typescript
// Open with optional chaining
modalizeRef.current?.open?.();

// Close with optional chaining
modalizeRef.current?.close?.();
```

### Issues Found

1. **Inconsistent initialization**: InvoiceCashbackSummary and Points use `null as unknown as PropsModalizeRef` while PointsDetail and MyOrdersCashback use simple `null`. Both compile but the type assertion is unnecessary.

2. **Type consistency mismatch**: The design-system-app's `PropsModalizeRef` likely doesn't perfectly match React's `RefObject<T>` at the type level, which is why Pattern 1 uses the type assertion workaround.

3. **No type safety loss**: The optional chaining (`?.`) pattern mitigates the type mismatch at runtime, but the type assertion approach is less clean.

### Recommendation

**InvoiceCashbackSummary follows a valid but verbose pattern.** The `null as unknown as PropsModalizeRef` assertion:
- ✅ Ensures proper type definition for the ref
- ✅ Handles the design-system-app type compatibility issue
- ✅ Works safely with optional chaining

However, **PointsDetail's simpler approach** (`null`) is also valid and cleaner if the PropsModalizeRef type properly extends RefObject semantics.

**The typing is correct following project patterns.** The choice between Pattern 1 (type assertion) and Pattern 2 (simple null) depends on your linter/TypeScript strictness settings. Both are functionally equivalent due to the optional chaining usage.</result>
</task-notification>

"}]},"uuid":"66b4fdac-605e-4b1a-b5b0-3fe2ef752627","timestamp":"2026-01-27T02:03:25.027Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/redux/ducks/rescue.ts","content":"import { Action } from 'redux';
import createReducer from '~/redux/store/createReducer';

enum RescueTypes {
  REQUEST = '@rescue/REQUEST',
  SUCCESS = '@rescue/SUCCESS',
  FAILED = '@rescue/FAILED',
  CLEAR = '@rescue/CLEAR',
}
export interface ResgateResponse {
  status: {
    message: string;
  };
  data?: {
    status: string;
  };
}
export interface RescuePayload {
  value: number;
  campaignId?: string;
  productCode?: string;
  description: string;
  participantId?: string;
}

export interface RescueReducerProps {
  isLoading: boolean;
  isError: boolean;
  status: ResgateResponse['status'] | undefined;
}

type State = RescueReducerProps;

const initialState: RescueReducerProps = {
  isLoading: false,
  isError: false,
  status: undefined,
};

const rescueReducer = createReducer(initialState, {
  [RescueTypes.REQUEST](state: State) {
    return {
      ...state,
      isLoading: true,
      isError: false,
    };
  },
  [RescueTypes.SUCCESS](state: State, { status }: { status: ResgateResponse['status'] }) {
    return {
      ...state,
      status,
      isLoading: false,
      isError: false,
    };
  },
  [RescueTypes.FAILED](state: State, { status }: { status?: ResgateResponse['status'] }) {
    return {
      ...state,
      status,
      isLoading: false,
      isError: true,
    };
  },
  [RescueTypes.CLEAR](): State {
    return initialState;
  },
});

type RescueActions = {
  rescueRequest: (data: {
    payload?: RescuePayload;
    onSuccess?: (status: ResgateResponse['status']) => void;
    onFailed?: (status?: ResgateResponse['status']) => void;
  }) => Action;
  rescueSuccess: (data: { status: ResgateResponse['status'] }) => Action;
  rescueFailed: (data: { status?: ResgateResponse['status'] }) => Action;
  rescueClear: () => Action;
};

export const rescueActions: RescueActions = {
  rescueRequest: (data: {
    payload?: RescuePayload;
    onSuccess?: (status: ResgateResponse['status']) => void;
    onFailed?: (status?: ResgateResponse['status']) => void;
  }) => ({
    type: RescueTypes.REQUEST,
    data,
  }),
  rescueSuccess: (data: { status: ResgateResponse['status'] }) => ({
    type: RescueTypes.SUCCESS,
    ...data,
  }),
  rescueFailed: (data: { status?: ResgateResponse['status'] }) => ({
    type: RescueTypes.FAILED,
    ...data,
  }),
  rescueClear: () => ({
    type: RescueTypes.CLEAR,
  }),
};

export default rescueReducer;

import { useShallow } from 'zustand/shallow';
import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { performRescue, clearRescue } from '../services';
import type { RescuePayload, RescueCallbacks } from '../models';
import { RescueStatus } from '../models';

export function useRescue() {
  const rescue = useRootStore(useShallow((state) => state.rescue));

  const rescueRequest = useCallback(
    async (payload: RescuePayload, callbacks?: RescueCallbacks) => {
      await performRescue(payload, callbacks);
    },
    []
  );

  const reset = useCallback(() => {
    clearRescue();
  }, []);

  return {
    ...rescue,
    isLoading: rescue.status === RescueStatus.LOADING,
    isError: rescue.status === RescueStatus.ERROR,
    isSuccess: rescue.status === RescueStatus.SUCCESS,
    rescueRequest,
    reset,
  };
}

"}]},"uuid":"95316817-a8b8-4ae6-80e3-8e8b184628fb","timestamp":"2026-01-27T02:03:32.963Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/services/services/rescue.service.ts","content":"import { useRootStore } from '~/store';
import { rescueApi } from '../api';
import { RescueStatus, RescuePayload, RescueCallbacks } from '../models';
import { getParticipantIdFromToken } from '../utils/participantId';

export async function performRescue(
  payload: RescuePayload,
  callbacks?: RescueCallbacks
): Promise<void> {
  const { rescueActions } = useRootStore.getState();

  try {
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.LOADING);

    const participantId = payload.participantId || await getParticipantIdFromToken();
    const rescuePayload: RescuePayload = {
      ...payload,
      participantId,
    };

    const response = await rescueApi.rescue(rescuePayload);

    if (response?.data?.status === 'OK' || response?.status?.message) {
      rescueActions.setResponseStatus(response.status);
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.SUCCESS);
      callbacks?.onSuccess?.(response.status);
    } else {
      rescueActions.setResponseStatus(response?.status);
      rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
      callbacks?.onFailed?.(response?.status);
    }
  } catch (error) {
    rescueActions.setError('Erro ao realizar resgate');
    rescueActions.setStatus(RescueStatus.ERROR);
    callbacks?.onFailed?.();
  }
}

export function clearRescue(): void {
  const { rescueActions } = useRootStore.getState();
  rescueActions.reset();
}

              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphL"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mt="nano"
              mb="xxs"
            >
              {formattedSelectedReais}
            </Text>

            <SeparatorHorizontal
              mb="xxs"
              bgColor='BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02'

            <Text

              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"

            >

            <Text

            />

            <Loading title="Aguarde..." visible={!!isRescueLoading} />
          </Box>
        </>

        <>
          {isRescueConcluded && !isRescueFailed && (
            <>

};

useState(false);
  const convertionPrice = pointsQuantity * conversionFactor;
  const convertionValue = new Intl.NumberFormat('pt-BR', {
    style: 'currency',
    currency: 'BRL',
  }).format(convertionPrice);

  const handleGoBack = () => {
    navigation.goBack();
  };

  const handleContinue = () => {
    eventSelectContent({
      content_type: 'cta',
      content_id: 'continuar',
      location_1: 'monetizacao',
      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
      location_3: 'desconto_fatura',
    });

    const reaisValue = conversionFactor * getParsePoints(pointsValue);

    navigation.navigate('InvoiceCashbackSummary', {
      pointsSelected: getParsePoints(pointsValue),
      reaisSelected: reaisValue,
      conversionFactor,
      productCode: route?.params?.productCode,
      campaingId: route?.params?.campaingId,
    });
  };

  const invoiceList = (): any[] => {
    const whiteShape = theme.colors.LIGHT_BASE_01;
    const fintechColor = theme.colors.SECONDARY_COLOR_02;

    return [
      {
        type: 'default',
        icon: 'icon-switch-vertical',
        iconColor: fintechColor,
        iconColorShape: whiteShape,
        description: `Cada ${pointsQuantity.toLocaleString(
          'pt-br'
        )} pontos valem ${convertionValue} no cashback`,
      },
      {
        type: 'default',
        icon: 'icon-calendar',
        iconColor: fintechColor,
        iconColorShape: whiteShape,
        description: 'Você define quanto cashback quer aplicar na sua fatura aberta',
      },
      {
        type: 'default',
        icon: 'icon-payment-invoice',
        iconColor: fintechColor,
        iconColorShape: whiteShape,
        description: 'Se o valor for maior que o da fatura, a diferença será aplicada nas próximas',
      },
      {
        type: 'default',
        icon: 'icon-clock',
        iconColor: fintechColor,
        iconColorShape: whiteShape,
        description: 'O cashback acontece em até 72h',
      },
    ];
  };
  const rate = useMemo(() => {
    if (conversionFactor && conversionFactor > 0) {
      return conversionFactor;
    }
    return 0;
  }, [conversionFactor]);

  const items = invoiceList
    ? invoiceList().map((item) => ({
        icon: item.icon,
        iconColor: item.iconColor,
        description: item.description,
        iconColorShape: item.iconColorShape,
        type: item.type,
      }))
    : [];

  const handleChangePoints = (text: string) => {
    const val = getParsePoints(text);
    const formatted = val ? getFormattedPoints(val) : '';
    setPointsValue(formatted);

    if (rate > 0) {
      setReaisValue(val > 0 ? formatCurrencyBR(val * rate) : '');
    }
    if (val > (Math.trunc(Number(points)) || 0)) {
      setPointsError(true);
    } else {
      setPointsError(false);
    }
  };

  const handleChangeReais = (text: string) => {
    const val = getParsedCurrency(text);
    const formatted = val > 0 ? formatCurrencyBR(val) : '';
    setReaisValue(formatted);

    if (rate > 0) {
      const ptsFromReais = Math.round(val / rate);
      setPointsValue(ptsFromReais > 0 ? getFormattedPoints(ptsFromReais) : '');
    }
    if (val > finalPrice) {
      setReaisError(true);
    } else {
      setReaisError(false);
    }
  };

  const handleUseAll = () => {
    setPointsValue(getFormattedPoints(points || 0));
    setReaisValue(formatCurrencyBR(finalPrice));
    setPointsError(false);
    setReaisError(false);
  };

  useFocusEffect(
    React.useCallback(() => {
      setPointsValue('');
      setReaisValue('');
      setPointsError(false);
      setReaisError(false);
      return () => {
        setPointsValue('');
        setReaisValue('');
        setPointsError(false);
        setReaisError(false);
      };
    }, [setPointsValue, setReaisValue])
  );

  const modalizeRef1 = useRef(null);

  const onOpenDefault = () => {
    eventSelectContent({
      content_type: 'cta',
      content_id: 'informacoes',
      location_1: 'monetizacao',
      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
      location_3: 'desconto_fatura',
    });

    modalizeRef1.current?.open();

    eventModalView({
      content_id: 'info_desconto_fatura',
      location_1: 'monetizacao',
      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
      location_3: 'desconto_fatura',
    });
  };

  const handleModalizeContinue = () => {
    eventSelectContent({
      content_type: 'cta',
      content_id: 'entendi',
      location_1: 'monetizacao',
      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
      location_3: 'info_desconto_fatura',
    });

    modalizeRef1.current?.close();
  };

  const handleSelectEvent = (contentId: string) => {
    eventSelectContent({
      content_type: 'form',
      content_id: contentId,
      location_1: 'monetizacao',
      location_2: 'hub_recompensas_spmais',
      location_3: 'desconto_fatura',
    });
  };

  return (
    <InvoiceCashbackPointsView
      handleGoBack={handleGoBack}
      cashbackInvoiceList={invoiceList}
      points={Math.trunc(Number(points)) || 0}
      handleContinue={handleContinue}
      price={pointsPrice}
      conversionFactor={conversionFactor}
      formattedPrice={formatCurrencyBR(finalPrice)}
      handleChangeReais={handleChangeReais}
      pointsValue={pointsValue}
      reaisValue={reaisValue}
      convertionValue={convertionValue}
      pointsQuantity={pointsQuantity}
      pointsError={pointsError}
      reaisError={reaisError}
      handleChangePoints={handleChangePoints}
      handleUseAll={handleUseAll}
      items={items}
      handleModalizeContinue={handleModalizeContinue}
      onOpenDefault={onOpenDefault}
      modalizeRef1={modalizeRef1}
      handleSelectEvent={handleSelectEvent}
      height={height}
      width={width}
      isLoadingParameterization={isLoadingParameterization}
    />
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPoints;

import { Dispatch } from 'redux';

import { AppReducers } from '../../redux/reducers/Models';
import { PointsTransaction } from '../../redux/ducks/points';

interface AnalyticsProps {
  analytics: {
    screen_name: string;
    screen_class: string;
    screen_bussines: string;
    screen_info: {
      location_1: string;
      location_2: string;
      location_3: string;
    };
  };
}

export type OptionalAnalyticsProps = Partial<AnalyticsProps> | undefined;

export type PropsFintechCardRoutes = {
  LearnMoreRewardProgram: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  RewardProgramMarketplace: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  AirlineMilesMarketplace: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  InvoiceCashbackOnboarding: {
    conversionFactor?: number;
    productCode?: string;
    campaingId?: string;
  } & OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  InvoiceCashbackPoints: {
    conversionFactor?: number;
    productCode?: string;
    campaingId?: string;
  } & OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  InvoiceCashbackSummary: {
    conversionFactor?: number;
    pointsSelected?: number;
    reaisSelected?: number;
    productCode?: string;
    campaingId?: string;
  } & OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  RewardProgram: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  PointsDetail: {
    transaction: PointsTransaction;
  } & OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  ExtractRewardProgram: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
  MyOrdersRewardProgram: OptionalAnalyticsProps;
};

export type PropsRootStack = PropsFintechCardRoutes;

export type ConnectProps<
  StateT extends (reducers: AppReducers) => object = () => object,
  DispatchT extends (dispatch: Dispatch) => object = () => object
> = ReturnType<StateT> & ReturnType<DispatchT>;

export type ScreenProps<
  RouteName extends keyof PropsRootStack,
  StateT extends (reducers: AppReducers) => object = () => object,
  DispatchT extends (dispatch: Dispatch) => object = () => object
> = ConnectProps<StateT, DispatchT> & NativeStackScreenProps<PropsRootStack, RouteName>;

<summary>Agent "Review usePoints integration" completed</summary>
<result>## Integration Review: usePoints Hook in InvoiceCashbackSummary Screen

### Summary
The `usePoints` hook integration in `InvoiceCashbackSummary` is **functionally correct but has a critical architectural issue** that impacts data flow.

---

## Findings

### ✅ Correct Integration Points

**1. Hook Usage (Line 24 in index.tsx)**
```typescript
const { conversionFactor } = usePoints();
```
- Correctly destructures `conversionFactor` from the hook
- Hook is imported from `../../services` (line 7)

**2. Hook Implementation (usePoints.ts)**
- Correctly retrieves `parameterPointsProgram` from root store using `useRootStore` (line 18-20)
- Extracts `conversionFactor` from state at line 57
- Returns it in the hook's public interface

**3. Data Structure Alignment**
- `ParameterPointsProgramState` defines `conversionFactor: number` (parameter.model.ts:33)
- Initial value: `0.1` (parameter.store.ts:27) — sensible default
- Store action `setConversionFactor` available for updates (parameter.store.ts:13)

**4. Usage in Calculation**
```typescript
// Line 57: index.tsx
const finalPrice = pts * conversionFactor;
```
Direct multiplication is correct for points-to-currency conversion.

---

## ⚠️ Critical Issue: Missing Data Population

**The conversionFactor is never populated from the API.**

**Root Cause:**
The `getParameterPointsProgram()` service (parameter.service.ts) only:
1. Fetches data from API (line 19)
2. Maps the response (line 22)
3. Calls `setData()` to store FAQ categories (line 23)
4. **NEVER calls `setConversionFactor()`**

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// parameter.service.ts lines 19-23
const response = await getParameterPointsProgramApi();
if (response.status === 200 && response?.data?.length > 0) {
  const data = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(response.data);
  parameterPointsProgramActions.setData(data);  // Only stores FAQ categories
  return;
}
```

**Dependency Tree Shows the Gap:**
- `InvoiceCashbackSummary` → uses `conversionFactor` from hook
- Hook → reads `parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor` from store
- Store → initialized with `0.1` (hardcoded default)
- Service → **never updates this value from API**

---

## Architectural Discrepancy

**InvoiceCashbackPoints screen (line 54-86) handles this differently:**
```typescript
const conversionFactor = route?.params?.conversionFactor ?? 0;
```
It expects `conversionFactor` passed via route params, not from Zustand store. This bypasses the missing population issue entirely.

**InvoiceCashbackSummary depends on:**
1. `InvoiceCashbackPoints` to pass it via route params (line 86 in InvoiceCashbackPoints)
2. Uses value from hook only for rendering description text
3. But this creates implicit coupling to route params

---

## Issues & Risks

| Issue | Severity | Impact |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Missing `setConversionFactor()` call in parameter service | HIGH | Will always use hardcoded 0.1; won't reflect actual backend rates |
| Hook returns undefined state field | MEDIUM | Type safety not violated but semantic issue |
| No error handling if conversionFactor is missing | MEDIUM | Silent fallback to 0.1 could cause wrong cashback calculations |
| Route param dependency not documented | MEDIUM | InvoiceCashbackSummary doesn't explicitly call `requestParameterization()` |

---

## Recommendations

1. **Populate conversionFactor from API response** - The backend must return this value; it needs to be extracted and stored
2. **Add explicit initialization** - InvoiceCashbackSummary should call `requestParameterization()` on mount (like InvoiceCashbackPoints does at line 31)
3. **Remove route param coupling** - Use store value consistently across all screens rather than mixing route params + store
4. **Add validation** - Verify conversionFactor is > 0 before calculations
5. **Document the data flow** - Add JSDoc explaining where conversionFactor comes from

The hook itself is correctly implemented, but the data population layer is incomplete.</result>
</task-notification>

### Summary
The `usePoints` hook integration in `InvoiceCashbackSummary` is **functionally correct but has a critical architectural issue** that impacts data flow.

---

## Findings

### ✅ Correct Integration Points

**1. Hook Usage (Line 24 in index.tsx)**
```typescript
const { conversionFactor } = usePoints();
```
- Correctly destructures `conversionFactor` from the hook
- Hook is imported from `../../services` (line 7)

**2. Hook Implementation (usePoints.ts)**
- Correctly retrieves `parameterPointsProgram` from root store using `useRootStore` (line 18-20)
- Extracts `conversionFactor` from state at line 57
- Returns it in the hook's public interface

**3. Data Structure Alignment**
- `ParameterPointsProgramState` defines `conversionFactor: number` (parameter.model.ts:33)
- Initial value: `0.1` (parameter.store.ts:27) — sensible default
- Store action `setConversionFactor` available for updates (parameter.store.ts:13)

**4. Usage in Calculation**
```typescript
// Line 57: index.tsx
const finalPrice = pts * conversionFactor;
```
Direct multiplication is correct for points-to-currency conversion.

---

## ⚠️ Critical Issue: Missing Data Population

**The conversionFactor is never populated from the API.**

**Root Cause:**
The `getParameterPointsProgram()` service (parameter.service.ts) only:
1. Fetches data from API (line 19)
2. Maps the response (line 22)
3. Calls `setData()` to store FAQ categories (line 23)
4. **NEVER calls `setConversionFactor()`**

**Evidence:**
```typescript
// parameter.service.ts lines 19-23
const response = await getParameterPointsProgramApi();
if (response.status === 200 && response?.data?.length > 0) {
  const data = mapParameterPointsProgramResponse(response.data);
  parameterPointsProgramActions.setData(data);  // Only stores FAQ categories
  return;
}
```

**Dependency Tree Shows the Gap:**
- `InvoiceCashbackSummary` → uses `conversionFactor` from hook
- Hook → reads `parameterPointsProgram.conversionFactor` from store
- Store → initialized with `0.1` (hardcoded default)
- Service → **never updates this value from API**

---

## Architectural Discrepancy

**InvoiceCashbackPoints screen (line 54-86) handles this differently:**
```typescript
const conversionFactor = route?.params?.conversionFactor ?? 0;
```
It expects `conversionFactor` passed via route params, not from Zustand store. This bypasses the missing population issue entirely.

**InvoiceCashbackSummary depends on:**
1. `InvoiceCashbackPoints` to pass it via route params (line 86 in InvoiceCashbackPoints)
2. Uses value from hook only for rendering description text
3. But this creates implicit coupling to route params

---

## Issues & Risks

| Issue | Severity | Impact |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Missing `setConversionFactor()` call in parameter service | HIGH | Will always use hardcoded 0.1; won't reflect actual backend rates |
| Hook returns undefined state field | MEDIUM | Type safety not violated but semantic issue |
| No error handling if conversionFactor is missing | MEDIUM | Silent fallback to 0.1 could cause wrong cashback calculations |
| Route param dependency not documented | MEDIUM | InvoiceCashbackSummary doesn't explicitly call `requestParameterization()` |

---

## Recommendations

1. **Populate conversionFactor from API response** - The backend must return this value; it needs to be extracted and stored
2. **Add explicit initialization** - InvoiceCashbackSummary should call `requestParameterization()` on mount (like InvoiceCashbackPoints does at line 31)
3. **Remove route param coupling** - Use store value consistently across all screens rather than mixing route params + store
4. **Add validation** - Verify conversionFactor is > 0 before calculations
5. **Document the data flow** - Add JSDoc explaining where conversionFactor comes from

The hook itself is correctly implemented, but the data population layer is incomplete."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":200,"cache_read_input_tokens":30496,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":200,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CXX4XzAq68kfQnsagogmS","type":"assistant","uuid":"7ac086cc-509b-4c1c-94fc-b98d5c38eb19","timestamp":"2026-01-27T02:04:06.247Z"}

<summary>Agent "Review logic preservation" completed</summary>
<result>Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive verification report.

## Business Logic Migration Verification Report

Based on my thorough analysis of the InvoiceCashbackSummary migration from Redux to Zustand, I have identified **critical business logic changes that were NOT preserved correctly**.

### Critical Finding: Logic Error in `isRescueConcluded`

**Old Logic (Redux - working correctly):**
```typescript
const isRescueLoading = rescueStatus === RescueStatus.LOADING;
const isRescueFailed = rescueStatus === RescueStatus.ERROR;
const isRescueConcluded = rescueStatus === RescueStatus.SUCCESS && !!rescueResponse;
```

**New Logic (Zustand - BROKEN):**
```typescript
const isRescueConcluded = !isRescueFailed && !!rescueStatus;
```

**The Problem:**

The new logic is fundamentally broken:

1. **Does not check for SUCCESS state**: The old code explicitly checks `rescueStatus === RescueStatus.SUCCESS` before considering it concluded. The new code only checks `!isRescueFailed` (i.e., status is NOT ERROR), which also matches LOADING and IDLE states.

2. **Inverted logic meaning**: The new condition `!isRescueFailed && !!rescueStatus` evaluates to true during LOADING state because:
   - `isRescueFailed = rescue.status === RescueStatus.ERROR` → false during LOADING
   - `!!rescueStatus` → true (the string "loading" is truthy)
   - Result: `true && true` → **TRUE** (WRONG - should be false during loading)

3. **Lost dependency on responseStatus**: The old code also verified `!!rescueResponse` existed before declaring success. The current Zustand state has `responseStatus` but the logic doesn't check it.

### State Structure Mismatch

**Old Redux state:**
```typescript
interface RescueState {
  status: RescueStatus;
  response?: RescueResponse['status'];  // setSuccess() populates this
  error?: string;
}
```

**New Zustand state:**
```typescript
interface RescueState {
  status: RescueStatus;
  responseStatus?: { message: string };  // setResponseStatus() populates this
  error?: string;
}
```

**Impact**: The hook returns `responseStatus` instead of checking the old `response` field, but the screen logic doesn't use this field name at all.

### What Should Be in the Screen

The correct logic should be:
```typescript
const isRescueConcluded = !isRescueFailed && rescue.status === RescueStatus.SUCCESS && !!rescueStatus;
```

Or better, destructure properly from the hook to match the Zustand API:
```typescript
const { 
  isLoading, 
  isError: isRescueFailed, 
  isSuccess,  // <-- missing in current code
  responseStatus 
} = useRescue();

const isRescueConcluded = isSuccess && !!responseStatus;
```

### Other Logic Changes Verified

✓ **Payload structure** - PRESERVED correctly
✓ **onConfirmRescue** - PRESERVED (both are sync now, calls rescueRequest)

  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
  Alert,
  Loading,
  SeparatorHorizontal,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import { FC } from 'react';

import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import React from 'react';

const InvoiceCashbackSummaryView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  onConfirmRescue,
  onExitResult,
  isRescueLoading,
  isRescueConcluded,
  isRescueFailed,
  items,
  effectiveShowResult,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  formattedSelectedPoints,
  formattedSelectedReais,
  screenTitle,
  height,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title={screenTitle}
      hasRightIcon={!effectiveShowResult}
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      {!effectiveShowResult ? (
        <>
          <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
            <Text
              color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
              testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
              type="headingMDL"
              mt="smaller"
              mb="small"
            >
              Revise as informações para confirmar o cashback
            </Text>

            <Text
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphMDM"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mb="nano"
            >
              Valor em pontos
            </Text>

            <Text
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphL"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mt="nano"
              mb="xxs"
            >
              {formattedSelectedPoints}
            </Text>

            <SeparatorHorizontal
              mb="xxs"
              bgColor='BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02'
              height={1}
              width="100%"
            />

            <Text
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphMDM"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mb="nano"
            >
              Valor em reais
            </Text>

            <Text
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphL"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mt="nano"
              mb="xxs"
            >
              {formattedSelectedReais}
            </Text>

            <SeparatorHorizontal
              mb="xxs"
              bgColor='BRAND_COMPLEMENT_02'
              height={1}
              width="100%"
            />

            <Text
              align="left"
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphMDM"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mb="nano"
            >
              Cashback em
            </Text>

            <Text
              color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              type="paragraphL"
              colorBold="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01"
              mt="nano"
              mb="xxs"
            >
              Até 72h
            </Text>

            <Alert
              label="O valor é aplicado e exibido nos lançamentos da sua fatura aberta."
              type="info"
              icon="icon-calendar"
            />
          </Box>

          <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
            <Button
              text="Confirmar desconto"
              mt="large"
              mb="small"
              onPress={onConfirmRescue}
              disabled={!!isRescueLoading}
              testID="invoice-cashback-button"
            />

            <Loading title="Aguarde..." visible={!!isRescueLoading} />
          </Box>
        </>
      ) : (
        <>
          {isRescueConcluded && !isRescueFailed && (
            <>
              <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="center" align="center">
                <Box
                  bgColor="PLUS_COLOR_03"
                  borderColor="PLUS_COLOR_01"
                  borderRadiusBottomLeft="medium"
                  pd="innerMedium"
                  borderRadiusBottomRight="medium"
                  borderRadiusTopRight="medium"
                  borderRadiusTopLeft="medium"
                  borderBottomWidth="small"
                  borderTopWidth="small"
                  borderLeftWidth="small"
                  borderRightWidth="small"
                  mb="small"
                >
                  <Icon name="icon-check" color="PLUS_COLOR_01" size="sm" />
                </Box>

                <Text type="subtitleXXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="small">Cashback aplicado</Text>

                <Text align="center" type="paragraphLG" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03" mb="small">O valor será aplicado em até 72h na sua fatura aberta.</Text>
              </Box>

              <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
                <Button
                  text="Ir para o início"
                  mt="large"
                  mb="small"
                  onPress={onExitResult}
                  testID="invoice-cashback-result-success-button"
                />
              </Box>
            </>
          )}
          {!isRescueConcluded && isRescueFailed && (
            <>
              <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="center" align="center">
                <Box
                  bgColor="DENY_COLOR_03"
                  borderColor="DENY_COLOR_01"
                  borderRadiusBottomLeft="medium"
                  pd="innerMedium"
                  borderRadiusBottomRight="medium"
                  borderRadiusTopRight="medium"
                  borderRadiusTopLeft="medium"
                  borderBottomWidth="small"
                  borderTopWidth="small"
                  borderLeftWidth="small"
                  borderRightWidth="small"
                  mb="small"
                >
                  <Icon name="icon-circle-alert" color="DENY_COLOR_01" size="sm" />
                </Box>

                <Text type="subtitleXXL" color="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_01" mb="small">Algo deu errado!</Text>

                <Text type="paragraphLG" color="INTENSE_COLOR_03" mb="small">Por favor, tente mais tarde.</Text>
              </Box>

              <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
                <Button
                  text="Voltar"
                  mt="large"
                  mb="small"
                  onPress={onExitResult}
                  testID="invoice-cashback-result-failed-button"
                />
              </Box>
            </>
          )}
        </>
      )}

      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={() => modalizeRef1.current?.close()} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackSummaryView;
